goal: Diagnose disconnected TF trees between Isaac Sim and Nav2, fix root-frame mismatch and sim-clock propagation.
thoughts: Two disconnected TF forests is almost always a root-frame naming issue (sim root='world', Nav2 root='map') plus sim-time drift across lifecycle nodes. Diagnose first (show_tf_tree), then decide: rename sim root vs publish static world->map TF. use_sim_time must be set on every lifecycle node individually — inheritance through launch args is unreliable. Never disable /tf_static.
code:
# 1. Inspect current TF state
tree = show_tf_tree(include_static=True)
if tree.roots == ["<SIM_ROOT>", "<NAV2_ROOT>"]:
    # Two-root case confirmed
    diag = diagnose_ros2(focus="tf", namespace="<NAMESPACE>")

# 2. Either rename sim root OR publish static transform
# Preferred: static world->map bridge
ros2_publish_sequence(
    topic="/tf_static",
    msg_type="tf2_msgs/msg/TFMessage",
    messages=[{"transforms": [{"header": {"frame_id": "<SIM_ROOT>"},
                                 "child_frame_id": "map",
                                 "transform": {"translation": [0,0,0], "rotation": [0,0,0,1]}}]}],
)

# 3. Fix sim-time propagation — must be per-lifecycle-node
configure_ros2_time(use_sim_time=True, nodes=["<NAV2_NODE_1>", "<NAV2_NODE_2>"])

# 4. Verify
fix_ros2_qos(topic="/tf", reliability="reliable", durability="volatile")
show_tf_tree(include_static=True)  # expect single connected tree
failure_modes: assistant sets use_sim_time only on top-level launch arg
assistant fabricates a non-existent Nav2 lifecycle node name
assistant confuses ROS clock with sim clock
qa_status: referenced; usage evidence in tests/test_phase12_qa.py, scripts/qa/run_all_sequential.py; classified 2026-05-15
